addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 3 accepted TASK-005 design update with no findings.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Implementation plan uses the accepted design as source of truth and records no Step 5 feedback for this run.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found the focused TASK-005 plan maps to the accepted design and has explicit deliverables, dependencies, completion criteria, progress tracking, and verification commands.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: No design revision or focused-plan revision required.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Focused TASK-005 plan was marked Ready while parent completed/swift-native-migration:TASK-002 and TASK-003 were still recorded as blockers.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Marked the focused TASK-005 plan Blocked, set phase 196 to BLOCKED, updated README status, and added an activation rule requiring parent TASK-002/TASK-003 completion or explicit stability acceptance before implementation starts.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Candidate-path work planned reader/validation behavior but omitted explicit runtime-owned provisioning, pre-attempt clearing, and post-attempt cleanup or ignore lifecycle.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added RuntimeCandidatePathStaging and RuntimeCandidatePathReservation planning, prepareCandidatePath/finalizeCandidatePath lifecycle expectations, checklist items, task deliverables, completion criteria, and rg verification commands.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: TASK-001 markdown status still said Not Started while PROGRESS.json marked it Blocked on parent TASK-002/TASK-003 dependencies.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Updated TASK-001 markdown status to Blocked so the focused plan, progress index, and activation rule agree before renewed Step 5 review.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Focused TASK-005 implementation was required after Step 5 accepted the plan and prior candidate-path lifecycle findings were addressed.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added runtime-owned Swift APIs and deterministic in-memory behavior for sessions, messages, candidate paths, output validation, publication, root output selection, append failures, and no-publication failure paths with no legacy inbox/outbox contracts.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review requested runtime publication boundary hardening for exact candidate-path ownership and adapter/provider failure handling.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects candidate-path submissions that do not match the exact reserved candidate path and exposes adapterFailure handling that marks the step failed without publishing messages. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 79 tests; TypeScript typecheck, Biome lint, and workflow validation passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found unsafe candidate staging path components before recursive cleanup and shallow Swift output-contract validation versus packages/riela-core/src/json-schema.ts.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime candidate staging now rejects unsafe path components and refuses outside-root finalization; Swift output validation now covers additionalProperties, enum, const, nested items/properties, numeric/string bounds, strict integer checks, and anyOf/oneOf/allOf. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 82 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found Swift output-contract validation validated payload values directly but did not validate schema definitions like packages/riela-core/src/json-schema.ts, allowing unsupported or malformed contract keywords to be silently ignored.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: DefaultWorkflowOutputValidator now validates schema definitions before payload validation, rejects unsupported keywords and malformed type/properties/required/additionalProperties/items/enum/bounds/pattern/combinator definitions, and has malformed-schema regression tests. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 83 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found candidate source precedence could bypass candidate-path reservation validation and publication did not finalize candidate-path staging after consumption.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects ambiguous candidate sources, requires candidate-path requests to use the reserved candidate path when a reservation is present, and finalizes candidate-path staging after runtime consumption. Added tests for adapterOutput+candidatePath ambiguity and cleanup on success, validation failure, and append failure. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 88 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found unsupported cross-workflow, resume-step, and fanout transition fields were silently converted into direct in-workflow messages, creating misleading successful publication.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects unsupported transition semantics before accepted output or message append. Added tests for toWorkflowId, resumeStepId, and fanout transitions proving no acceptedOutput and no workflow messages. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 89 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found prior WorkflowMessageRecord rows were not resolved into one structured adapter/executor input object and root output selection used transitions.isEmpty, allowing any terminal step to publish external workflow output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added runtime-owned WorkflowResolvedMessageInput and DefaultWorkflowMessageInputResolver with deterministic ordering/filtering/merge tests and AdapterExecutionInput application. Added explicit publishesRootOutput metadata so terminal non-output steps do not publish rootOutput. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 91 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found candidate-path staging could follow safe-named symlink components outside the staging root and message input resolution consumed created, failed, or superseded workflow message rows.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Candidate staging now validates existing path components before creation, validates the resolved staging directory after creation, and rejects symlink escapes without returning outside-root candidate paths. Runtime message append now stores direct messages as delivered, and DefaultWorkflowMessageInputResolver includes only delivered or consumed messages. Added symlink escape and lifecycle filtering regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 93 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found TASK-006 Swift package manifest metadata and event validation parity gaps versus the TypeScript package and event contracts.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added TS-compatible manifest metadata fields, dependency string decoding, required metadata/checksum/add-on safety validation, file-change and s3-repository event contracts, template reference validation, and output destination validation. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 113 tests.
